name: Sourdough Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
Few people bake cakes from scratch anymore unless they can produce
something that's not possible with a mix. This sourdough chocolate cake
falls in that category. It's got a fudge-like quality you can't get any
way except with sourdough and since it's all mixed in one bowl, it's almost
as easy as a boxed cake.
1. Put the starter in a large bowl, cover loosely and allow to stand at room
temperature until active and bubbling -- at least one hour.
2. Add the rest of the ingredients, in the order given, beating well after each
addition. It is not necessary to sift or premix any of the ingredients as
long as you are careful to get the baking powder and soda evenly mixed.
3. Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans.
4. Pour the batter into the prepared pans. The batter will be thin.
5. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for about 30 minutes, or until the layers
test done when poked with a toothpick. The cake will have pulled away from
the sides of the pan.
6. Allow to cool about 10 minutes before removing from pans.
7. Finish cooling the layers on wire racks.
8. Do not frost until the cake is completely cold.
9. A mild chocolate butter cream frosting is nice with this cake.
